asserts: handoff-style command graph applies successfully
transaction path advances revision
revision mismatch blocks apply
blocked stage classified as revision
reduced-confidence warning emitted
validation-ready plan still produced
prior manual timing ownership does not suppress later timing writes
timing edits are cumulative sequence state
partial scope planning remains deterministic
scope metadata narrows to selected target/section

intent: use production xLights sequences as a functionality corpus only
do not reward imitation of any one user's visual style or pacing
prefer capability correctness over corpus frequency when the manual documents valid behavior
commonMechanics: layer methods including Layered, Average, masking, and reveal modes
in/out transition coverage including Wipe and Circle Explode
per-effect buffer styles such as Per Preview, Per Model Default, Single Line, and Per Model Per Preview
2D buffer transforms such as Flip Horizontal and Rotate CC 90
timing-track vocabulary variability including Beats, Bars, Note Onsets, metronome tracks, and song-specific names
group and submodel targeting patterns, including dense submodel-heavy props
rareButDocumentedMechanics: rare layer modes such as Additive, Subtractive, Brightness, and Shadow variants
rare transitions such as Doorway, Circular Swirl, Blinds, Star, Square Explode, and Slide Bars
documented per-effect buffer behavior such as Persistent

asserts: sequence_agent recognizes common layer methods as valid shared timing/layer settings
planning does not warn on corpus-backed shared settings without cause
sequence_agent accepts common per-effect buffer style and transform settings as valid effect settings
planning remains style-neutral and does not prefer one buffer style by default
sequence_agent does not require canonical beats or lyrics track names
timing-track handling remains semantics-driven instead of name-biased
sequence_agent treats dense submodel target lists as standard xLights functionality
planning preserves explicit submodel targets without collapsing them to the parent model
documented rare transition settings are treated as valid when requested
lack of corpus frequency does not cause rejection of documented xLights functionality
evals do not reward matching historical user sequence style
success is based on valid xLights functionality and safe deterministic behavior

notes: Eval assets are functionality-oriented and should not reward stylistic imitation.
Historical user sequences may be referenced as capability corpus only, not aesthetic ground truth.
Sequence-agent evals should remain focused on sequencing functionality and execution safety, not designer-side creative composition.
